relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Theft requires more than return; harm must be repaired.
Night intrusion and bloodguilt are distinguished.
Daylight changes the judgment; theft still requires restitution.
Found stolen life is repaid double.
Careless grazing must be restored from the best.
Fire that spreads creates responsibility.
Guarded goods stolen require double restitution from the thief.
When the thief is unknown, the matter comes near Divine.
Disputed property comes before Divine judgment.
Entrusted animals require truth when harm occurs unseen.
An oath before YHWH resolves hidden responsibility.
If stolen from the guardian, repayment follows.
A torn animal becomes witness.
Borrowed goods harmed without owner require repayment.
Owner presence changes liability.
Seduction law reveals patriarchal protection structures needing audit.
The father may refuse, but compensation remains.
Ancient sorcery prohibition is severe and dangerous in reception.
Bestial misuse is judged severely.
Sacrifice belongs to YHWH alone.
Do not wrong the sojourner, because you know Egypt.
Do not afflict widow or orphan.
If they cry, YHWH will surely hear.
The oppressor may become what he created: widowhood and orphanhood.
Do not lend to the poor like a devouring creditor.
Return the cloak before night.
The poor one's covering is survival, and YHWH hears because he is gracious.
Do not curse Divine or leader.
Do not delay the fullness offered to YHWH.
Even firstborn animals remain with mother seven days before offering.
Holiness shapes what the people consume.

contemplative_note: Exodus 22 presses covenant into daily repair: restitution, entrusted goods, sojourner dignity, widow and orphan protection, no predatory lending, and the gracious Divine who hears the poor.
